Title: Cell-type-specific response to silicon treatment in soybean leaves revealed by single-nucleus RNA sequencing and targeted gene editing

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Silicon efflux transporter; anion transporter superfamily; rice OsSiet4-related family/QTL candidate on Chr.16
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fortifies defense responses; mitigates stress; contributes to biotic stress tolerance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-specific transporter; higher in roots than leaves by qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-specific; functional effect detected through root editing; not leaf-specific like GmHiSil2c
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GmUbi constitutive promoter for Cas9; CmYLCV promoter for gRNAs in CRISPR constructs
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydroponic culture; 1.7 mM Si (K2SiO3/Kasil 6) treatment; composite hairy root CRISPR assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes root-to-shoot Si uptake/translocation; knockout in roots significantly reduces leaf Si accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in composite soybean hairy roots; amplicon sequencing; pXRF measurement of leaf Si; Xenopus oocyte Ge(OH)4 efflux assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmHiSil2b encodes an active Si efflux transporter with moderate Ge/Si analog efflux activity in Xenopus oocytes. 2. Root-specific CRISPR knockout of GmHiSil2b significantly reduced Si accumulation in leaves, demonstrating a causal role in root Si uptake/translocation. 3. The gene acts in a tissue-specific Si transporter network distinct from the leaf-expressed GmHiSil2c.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to tune soybean Si uptake from roots and enhance stress-resilience traits linked to Si accumulation; root-focused editing or allele selection may modulate Si loading to shoots.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although stronger efflux activity was observed for GmHiSil2c in oocytes, only GmHiSil2b root knockout reduced leaf Si because the composite editing system targeted roots while shoots remained wild type.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Silicon efflux transporter; homolog of rice OsSiet4; anion transporter superfamily/QTL candidate on Chr.16
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf epidermis and leaf tissues where Si is deposited in patches between epidermis and cuticle and around trichomes
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fortifies defense responses; mitigates stress; contributes to biotic stress tolerance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf-specific/leaf-enriched; exclusively expressed in leaf epidermal cells upon Si treatment; low/no relevant root effect in composite root assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Exclusively expressed in leaf epidermal cells after Si treatment
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CmYLCV constitutive promoter for GFP localization assay; GmUbi constitutive promoter for Cas9 and CmYLCV for gRNAs in CRISPR constructs
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydroponic culture; 1.7 mM Si treatment; leaf snRNA-seq; Xenopus oocyte assay; root-only composite hairy root CRISPR assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a leaf-specific Si efflux transporter induced by Si treatment, likely mediating Si efflux/distribution in leaf epidermal cells
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: snRNA-seq expression profiling; qRT-PCR; GFP subcellular localization in N. benthamiana protoplasts; Xenopus oocyte Ge(OH)4 efflux assay; CRISPR/Cas9 editing in composite hairy roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmHiSil2c is strongly induced by Si and specifically expressed in soybean leaf epidermal cells. 2. The protein localizes to the plasma membrane and shows strong Ge/Si analog efflux activity in Xenopus oocytes (~70% reduction). 3. Root knockout did not alter leaf Si accumulation, consistent with its leaf-specific role rather than root uptake function.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering leaf Si distribution/homeostasis to improve stress tolerance without directly altering root uptake; tissue-specific manipulation may be required.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stable edited mutations were recovered in T0 plants but not transmitted to the next generation, suggesting possible biological constraints related to Si homeostasis; root-only knockout showed no phenotype on leaf Si.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The absence of a phenotype in root-edited composite plants does not negate function; it reflects that GmHiSil2c is primarily leaf-expressed and the editing assay affected roots only.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in control (Si−) leaves, particularly epidermal cells; decreased after Si treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in leaf epidermal cells under Si− condition; downregulated after Si treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate Si influx and optimize Si nutrition/homeostasis in soybean.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Silicon-associated stress resilience and defense fortification
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CmYLCV constitutive promoter for GFP localization assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates influx/passive transport of silicic acid [Si(OH)4] across the plasma membrane
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for transport/supply of the target mineral nutrient
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydroponic 1.7 mM Si treatment versus KCl control
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NOD26-like intrinsic protein (NIP) aquaporin family; Lsi1-type silicon influx transporter
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression decreased under Si treatment, suggesting feedback or gradient-based control of Si influx.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to Si influx into leaf cells; expression is repressed after Si accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: snRNA-seq; qRT-PCR; GFP localization in protoplasts
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmNIP2-1 is a putative Lsi1-type Si influx transporter expressed in soybean leaves. 2. Its transcript is higher in control leaves, especially epidermal cells, and decreases after Si treatment. 3. Plasma membrane localization supports a transporter role in Si influx/homeostasis.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmNIP2-2 is a putative Lsi1-type Si influx transporter expressed in soybean leaves. 2. Its transcript is higher in control leaves, especially epidermal cells, and decreases after Si treatment. 3. Plasma membrane localization supports a transporter role in Si influx/homeostasis.
